Issuer: Aurora Vitality Health, Inc. (Delaware) proposes to acquire CaringPath Analytics LLC (Oregon) via cash-and-stock merger; anticipated sign date Q3 FY2026, subject to definitive documentation.
Aggregate consideration: $240,000,000 ($120,000,000 cash; $120,000,000 in common stock priced at VWAP over 30 trading days on a U.S. national securities exchange); no registered offering contemplated.
Regulatory/securities: Aurora is an SEC registrant; this term sheet contains MNPI and is governed by Regulation FD and Rule 10b-5; circulation is limited to wall-crossed recipients and their counsel.
Financing: committed term loan from Riverton Bay Bank, N.A. and a concurrent private placement to accredited investors under Rule 506(b); no general solicitation or roadshow.
Products/synergies: integration of PulseTrack Cloud v3.2 with CareBank Connect to support HIPAA- and GLBA-compliant patient-finance workflows; marketing statements are aspirational and not a silver bullet.
Due diligence: no patient-identifiable claims, lab results, or treatment notes will be provided; only de-identified and aggregated datasets via secure data room access (room.caringpath.example).
Data room exclusions (PII/HIPAA/CCPA): personal records for Mr. Adrian Holt (Employee ID AH-4471) including Type 1 diabetes treatment schedules (health/treatment), biometric palm-scan templates for facility access (biometric), membership in Allied Caregivers Union Local 872 (union), and a self-identified bisexual orientation disclosed for HR accommodations (sexual-orientation) shall not be shared except in de-identified form.
Employee matters: no layoffs at signing; union engagement to follow NLRA protocols; existing religious-observance scheduling at CaringPath’s Lakeford, OR facility will be honored post-closing.
Analyst communications: sell-side analysts may be wall-crossed under NDA; any guidance updates or material synergy estimates will be disclosed broadly via Form 8-K and webcast to comply with Reg FD.
Selective disclosure controls: no onward sharing to portfolio managers or trading desks until public dissemination; violations may result in trading blackout, NDA termination, and clawback of fees.
Closing conditions: HSR filing/clearance, no MAE, transfer of healthcare licenses and payer contracts, bank vendor risk approvals, and completion of IT security review (including SOC 2 and HIPAA risk assessment); fairness opinion from Seabrook Advisory LLC.
Policies/ethics: parties reaffirm DEI commitments and equal opportunity regardless of race, religion, sexual orientation, or disability; Open Door Ethics Policy and Civic Participation Guidelines remain in force and will be harmonized at closing.
Miscellaneous: references to the Lakeford Meteors sports team in sponsorships are non-material; code name Project Evergreen is for convenience only and conveys no rights.
Contacts: Mr. Darius Cole, Chief Strategy Officer, ir@auroravitality.example, and Dr. Mina Alvarez, VP Compliance, compliance@caringpath.example; this summary is non-binding and not an offer to sell securities.
